file_name: Apollo Endosurgery - Manufacturing and Supply Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: 1. DEFINITIONS 1.1"Affiliates" of a Party shall mean any corporation or other business entity controlling, controlled by, or under common control with such Party. 1.2"Certificate of Conformance" or "COC" shall mean a document prepared by ESTABLISHMENT containing at a minimum: product name, Lot (defined below) number, lot quantity and a statement indicating compliance to all product specifications. Each COC shall be signature approved by ESTABLISHMENT's Quality Assurance department. 1.3"Control" (including "controlling", "controlled by" and "under common control with" of any party, corporation, or other business entity) shall mean the direct or indirect ownership of at least fifty percent (50%) of the voting or income interest in such party, corporation, or other business entity, respectively. -1- 1.4"Current Good Manufacturing Practices" (abbreviated "GMPs" or "cGMPs") shall mean, a) for any period during the Term during which ESTABLISHMENT has received FDA certification, the standards established by the United States Food and Drug Administration (the "FDA") for current Good Manufacturing Practices, as specified in FDA 21 C.F.R. §820 Quality Systems Regulations (or its successor provisions); and b) ISO 13485 Medical Devices - Quality Management Systems and other sections so designated by the title "Good Manufacturing Practices"; and c) as applicable to each respective Product to be manufactured and/or supplied by ESTABLISHMENT.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. DEFINITIONS 1.1"Affiliates" of a Party shall mean any corporation or other business entity controlling, controlled by, or under common control with such Party. 1.2"Certificate of Conformance" or "COC" shall mean a document prepared by ESTABLISHMENT containing at a minimum: product name, Lot (defined below) number, lot quantity and a statement indicating compliance to all product specifications. Each COC shall be optionally approved by ESTABLISHMENT's Quality Assurance department. 1.3"Control" (including "controlling", "controlled by" and "under common control with" of any party, corporation, or other business entity) shall mean the direct or indirect ownership of at least fifty percent (50%) of the voting or income interest in such party, corporation, or other business entity, respectively. -1- 1.4"Current Good Manufacturing Practices" (abbreviated "GMPs" or "cGMPs") shall mean, a) for any period during the Term during which ESTABLISHMENT has received FDA certification, the standards established by the United States Food and Drug Administration (the "FDA") for current Good Manufacturing Practices, as specified in FDA 21 C.F.R. §820 Quality Systems Regulations (or its successor provisions); and b) ISO 13485 Medical Devices - Quality Management Systems and other sections so designated by the title "Good Manufacturing Practices"; and c) as applicable to each respective Product to be manufactured and/or supplied by ESTABLISHMENT.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'shall be signature approved' to 'shall be optionally approved' introduces ambiguity regarding the necessity of Quality Assurance approval on the Certificate of Conformance (COC). This contradicts the intention of section 1.2, because if there is no signature by ESTABLISHMENT's Quality Assurance Department, there is no assurance of compliance to all product specifications, making the requirement of 'compliance to all product specifications' in the statement weaker.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2"Certificate of Conformance" or "COC" shall mean a document prepared by ESTABLISHMENT containing at a minimum: product name, Lot (defined below) number, lot quantity and a statement indicating compliance to all product specifications. Each COC shall be optionally approved by ESTABLISHMENT's Quality Assurance department.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the Certificate of Conformance (COC) 'shall be signature approved,' ensuring Quality Assurance department's approval. The changed text makes this approval 'optionally approved,' which weakens the assurance of compliance to all product specifications, contradicting the intended purpose of the COC as a document guaranteeing conformance. The contradicted text, being the same as the changed text, highlights this contradiction. The alteration from a mandatory to an optional requirement introduces ambiguity and undermines the reliability of the COC, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. FORECASTS, PURCHASE ORDERS AND DELIVERY 5.1Forecasts. APOLLO shall provide ESTABLISHMENT on a monthly basis a twelve (12) month rolling forecast to allow for visibility into expected future demands. APOLLO shall deliver to ESTABLISHMENT a forecast for anticipated monthly deliveries of Product to APOLLO over the subsequent four (4) calendar quarters (the "Forecast"). The Forecast is to be used by the Parties for planning purposes and is not a commitment by APOLLO to purchase the quantities of Products specified in such Forecast, except as described below. The quantities of Product forecasted for the initial three (3) months of each updated rolling Forecast shall represent a binding obligation of Apollo to purchase from ESTABLISHMENT, and of ESTABLISHMENT to manufacture and supply to APOLLO, such quantities of Product. -5- ESTABLISHMENT shall, at all times during the Term, maintain an inventory of raw materials and components sufficient to manufacture the binding obligations.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. FORECASTS, PURCHASE ORDERS AND DELIVERY 5.1Forecasts. APOLLO shall provide ESTABLISHMENT on a monthly basis a twelve (12) month rolling forecast to allow for visibility into expected future demands. APOLLO shall deliver to ESTABLISHMENT a forecast for anticipated monthly deliveries of Product to APOLLO over the subsequent four (4) calendar quarters (the "Forecast"). The Forecast is to be used by the Parties for planning purposes and may not be a commitment by APOLLO to purchase the quantities of Products specified in such Forecast, except as described below. The quantities of Product forecasted for the initial three (3) months of each updated rolling Forecast shall represent a binding obligation of Apollo to purchase from ESTABLISHMENT, and of ESTABLISHMENT to manufacture and supply to APOLLO, such quantities of Product. -5- ESTABLISHMENT shall, at all times during the Term, maintain an inventory of raw materials and components sufficient to manufacture the binding obligations.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'is not a commitment' to 'may not be a commitment', we introduce a contradiction. Initially, the text clearly states the forecast is *not* a commitment, except for the first three months. Now, the change to 'may not be' opens the possibility that even forecasts beyond the initial three months could be considered a commitment under certain unstated conditions, hence the ambiguity. The requirement for ESTABLISHMENT to maintain inventory sufficient to meet binding obligations then becomes vague, as there is not a clear understanding if these inventories will satisfy all forecasted demands or just the ones falling in the first three months of each updated rolling Forecast.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1Forecasts. APOLLO shall provide ESTABLISHMENT on a monthly basis a twelve (12) month rolling forecast to allow for visibility into expected future demands. APOLLO shall deliver to ESTABLISHMENT a forecast for anticipated monthly deliveries of Product to APOLLO over the subsequent four (4) calendar quarters (the "Forecast"). The Forecast is to be used by the Parties for planning purposes and may not be a commitment by APOLLO to purchase the quantities of Products specified in such Forecast, except as described below. The quantities of Product forecasted for the initial three (3) months of each updated rolling Forecast shall represent a binding obligation of Apollo to purchase from ESTABLISHMENT, and of ESTABLISHMENT to manufacture and supply to APOLLO, such quantities of Product.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'is not a commitment' to 'may not be a commitment' introduces ambiguity about whether the forecast is a binding obligation. This contradicts the subsequent sentence stating that 'The quantities of Product forecasted for the initial three (3) months of each updated rolling Forecast shall represent a binding obligation,' because it is unclear whether the remaining nine months of forecast are binding. The Contradicted Text (which also occurs in the changed text) and Changed Text creates an inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: 7. REGULATORY AND QUALITY 7.1Compliance. ESTABLISHMENT agrees that its work under this Agreement will be conducted in compliance with all applicable laws, rules and regulations, and with the standard of care customary in the industry. If requested by APOLLO, ESTABLISHMENT shall provide APOLLO with a certificate evidencing its accreditation by the appropriate accrediting body. Such accreditation shall remain in force during the term of this Agreement. ESTABLISHMENT agrees that all Product shipments to APOLLO shall be in accordance with APOLLO's instructions governing the shipment, labeling, and packaging of the Product.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. REGULATORY AND QUALITY 7.1Compliance. ESTABLISHMENT agrees that its work under this Agreement will be conducted in compliance with all applicable laws, rules and regulations, and with a consideration of the standard of care customary in the industry. If requested by APOLLO, ESTABLISHMENT shall provide APOLLO with a certificate evidencing its accreditation by the appropriate accrediting body. Such accreditation shall remain in force during the term of this Agreement. ESTABLISHMENT agrees that all Product shipments to APOLLO shall be in accordance with APOLLO's instructions governing the shipment, labeling, and packaging of the Product.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will be conducted in compliance with... the standard of care' to 'will be conducted in compliance with... a consideration of the standard of care' weakens ESTABLISHMENT's obligation. Originally, ESTABLISHMENT *must* comply with the industry standard. Now, they only need to *consider* it, which provides leeway to deviate from the standard, contradicting later requirements for strict regulatory and quality adherence, creating an in-text contradiction. As the agreement mentions in the title and later, that APOLLO needs to be manufactured and supplied by ESTABLISHMENT, there would be an impact on what needs to be done by APOLLO as well, which will result in a confusion. Also, this affects Section 7.2 Quality Control, which now has no clear way of checking the quality based on Applicable Laws, quality system requirements communicated in writing by APOLLO.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.2Quality Control. Establishment shall maintain and follow a quality control and testing program consistent with the Product Specifications, ISO 13485:2012, Applicable Laws and quality system requirements communicated in writing by APOLLO from time to time -7- (the "Quality Control Procedures"). All Product supplied to APOLLO hereunder shall be manufactured in compliance with ISO 13485:2012 and all other applicable requirements of Regulatory Authorities, and in compliance with all other Applicable Laws (collectively, "Regulatory Standards"). At all times the Products shall be manufactured in an ISO Class 7 Clean Room, unless otherwise set forth in an amendment to this Agreement or the Exhibits hereto signed by both Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligation of ESTABLISHMENT from 'compliance with the standard of care customary in the industry' to only requiring 'a consideration of the standard of care.' This contradicts section 7.2, which requires ESTABLISHMENT to maintain a quality control and testing program consistent with ISO 13485:2012 and Applicable Laws, as well as manufacturing products in compliance with these Regulatory Standards. By only requiring a consideration of the industry standard, the changed text introduces ambiguity and allows for deviation from the strict compliance requirements outlined in section 7.2, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
